core_model.structure_summary: An invertible linear change of basis on a function defined over a Cartesian product of finite index sets, producing signed-sum coordinates that isolate main and higher-order interaction components, together with the minimal linear dependence relations (circuits) among the raw values.
core_model.canonical_form: c_S = \sum_{v} \chi_S(v)\, w_v, \quad \chi_S(v)=\prod_{i} \phi_i(v_i); \qquad w_{11} = w_{10} + w_{01} - w_{00} \;\; (\text{a rank-one / circuit relation})

variables.meaning: values assigned to each point of a Cartesian product of finite index sets
transformed coordinates (signed linear combinations) measuring main effects and higher-order interactions
fixed orthogonal-type sign/weight vectors defining the linear transform per coordinate

notes: Static algebraic structure: an orthogonal (Walsh/Hadamard-type) linear transform decomposing a function on a product lattice of alleles into interaction coordinates, plus minimal linear dependence relations ('circuits'). Few controlled-vocabulary tags fit cleanly; 'pairwise_coupling' captures the interaction-coordinate emphasis and the vanishing signed sums act like linear conservation constraints. No dynamics.
